No Poverty.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to the eradication of poverty or related targets.

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to ending hunger, food security, or sustainable agriculture.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to health, well-being, or related medical/public health topics.

Quality Education.reason: The course provides tertiary education (Target 4.3) and equips students with 'indispensable basic ability' in programming, directly enhancing relevant technical skills for employment and problem-solving (Target 4.4).

Gender Equality.reason: The course teaches 'Information technology' and 'Programming,' which are enabling technologies. This indirectly contributes to Target 5.b by providing skills that can promote the empowerment of women.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to water, sanitation, or hygiene.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to energy access, renewable energy, or energy efficiency.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course provides programming skills, described as an 'indispensable basic ability for all industries,' directly contributing to higher economic productivity through technological upgrading (Target 8.2) and preparing students for decent work.

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course directly enhances technological capabilities and innovation (Target 9.5) by teaching programming, data science, and problem-solving using information technology, aligning with the 'Speculation and Innovation Capacity' core competence.

Reduced Inequalities.reason: By specifically targeting students 'who do not yet have a basic foundation in programming,' the course promotes equal opportunity and reduces skill inequalities, aligning with Target 10.3.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to urban development, housing, transport, or cultural heritage.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to sustainable consumption, resource efficiency, or waste reduction.

Climate Action.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to climate change mitigation, adaptation, or awareness.

Life Below Water.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to marine ecosystems, ocean health, or sustainable fisheries.

Life on Land.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to terrestrial ecosystems, biodiversity, or deforestation.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to peace, justice, governance, or human rights.

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course enhances the use of 'information and communications technology' (ICT) by teaching programming, directly contributing to capacity building in enabling technology as per Target 17.8.
